events.title: Book Presentation Publics in Action: The Self-Making of Civic Life
events.summary: Combining political philosophy and comparative politics, this book explores how publics are formed and sustain democratic life. Through examples from the United States, France, and Norway, Christopher Kutz highlights the collective practices that make civic institutions possible.

events.title: European economic security in an age of de-risking
events.summary: In-person conference. Drawing on US, Japanese, and Chinese insights, this talk assesses Europe's current de-risking strategies to propose a more constructive, network-aware approach to economic security and supply chain resilience.

events.title: Three Nations, One Table: North American Agriculture under USMCA
events.institution_full: Center for Strategic and International Studies
events.summary: Three Nations, One Table: North American Agriculture under USMCA Join the Americas Program for a conference on USMCA and the future of North American food and agriculture. events.title: Launch of the French-Brazilian AURELA Research Project
events.summary: AURELA is a four-year Franco-Brazilian research program funded by CAPES-COFECUB and bringing together six universities in Brazil and France. The program explores key challenges in EU–Latin America relations, focusing on sustainability, security and emerging technologies, and democratic governance.
